theme: wiring the cheapest polynomial multiplier chip
title: Cheapest Polynomial Multiplier Chip
strategies: trivial: schoolbook (d+1)^2 products = the checker baseline
greedy: recursive Karatsuba (field-independent; leaves products on the table)
strong: CRT over F_p[x] -- linear moduli (field points, cost 1) plus irreducible quadratic moduli (cost 3, cover 2) to approach the 2d+1 rank bound
open headroom: exact bilinear rank of poly-mult over small F_p is unknown; better mixed-moduli schemes may beat the reference
